Done. Here's what I did:

## Docs Restructuring — Complete

### What was wrong (the roast)

**1. 33 loose files at `docs/` root** — CSVs, GeoJSONs, HTML checklists, startup deals (not even Nexa-related), all competing with actual documentation. No one could find anything.

**2. Pricing was dangerously wrong everywhere** — `pricing-matrix-complete.md` (486 lines) and `financial-model.md` (295 lines) assumed $2,900-$6,900 with 4 program tiers. Reality is $1,500 + commissions. The financial model projected $270K-$540K/yr — reality is ~$18K-$36K. Costs estimated at $10K-$18K/mo — reality is ~$0 fixed. **These files would mislead anyone making business decisions.**

**3. Contradictions between docs** — SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md said $1,500, everything else said $2,900-$6,900. README.md pointed to a path that doesn't exist. Site audit recommended features Sonia explicitly rejected.

**4. No "what changed" document** — someone reading the docs had to cross-reference 10+ files and detect version conflicts themselves. No deprecation warnings, no changelog.

**5. Duplicate deployment directories** — `00-deploy/` and `10-deployment/` with overlapping content.

### What I fixed

| Action | Files affected |
|--------|---------------|
| **Created CHANGELOG.md** — every assumption invalidated by May 11, with before/after/reason | New file |
| **Created DOCS_CRITIQUE.md** — full architectural critique with recommendations | New file |
| **Deprecation banners** on pricing-matrix, financial-model, and site-audit | 3 files |
| **Moved 33 root files** into proper subdirectories (01-client/, 09-market-intelligence/, etc.) | 33 files |
| **Moved 11 HTML deliverables** to `docs/deliverables/` | 11 files |
| **Fixed README.md** path + updated all cross-references | README.md |
| **Updated SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md** — §10, §11 hierarchy, §13 integrity checks | SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md |
| **Created `docs/meetings/`** directory for meeting reports | 2 files |
| **Committed + pushed** to github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay | 48 files, main branch |

### How to read docs now

1. `docs/CHANGELOG.md` — what changed and why
2. `docs/meetings/meeting-report-may-11.md` — the real pricing + brand outcome
3. `docs/SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md` — master client profile
4. Everything else in proper subdirectories

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

Do NOT capture (these become persistent self-imposed constraints that bite you later when the environment changes):
  • Environment-dependent failures: missing binaries, fresh-install errors, post-migration path mismatches, 'command not found', unconfigured credentials, uninstalled packages. The user can fix these — they are not durable rules.
  • Negative claims about tools or features ('browser tools do not work', 'X tool is broken', 'cannot use Y from execute_code'). These harden into refusals the agent cites against itself for months after the actual problem was fixed.
  • Session-specific transient errors that resolved before the conversation ended. If retrying worked, the lesson is the retry pattern, not the original failure.
  • One-off task narratives. A user asking 'summarize today's market' or 'analyze this PR' is not a class of work that warrants a skill.

If a tool failed because of setup state, capture the FIX (install command, config step, env var to set) under an existing setup or troubleshooting skill — never 'this tool does not work' as a standalone constraint.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.
Let me review the session for skill-worthy signals.

messages.content:
{"success": true, "name": "nexa-paraguay-deployment", "description": "Build and deploy the Nexa Paraguay site to Docker Swarm on VPS 72.61.44.159", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
name: nexa-paraguay-deployment
description: Build and deploy the Nexa Paraguay site to Docker Swarm on VPS 72.61.44.159
---

# Nexa Paraguay Deployment

## Current State (May 11 2026)

### Hybrid Dependencies
Sections, i18n, and client-kit stay on `.tgz` (the published `@ai-whisperers/sections@0.3.1` has a different API). All other `@ai-whisperers/*` come from GitHub Packages:
- `auth@0.2.0`, `admin@0.2.1`, `ui@0.4.0`, `seo@0.2.0`, `whatsapp@0.2.0`, `theme@0.2.0` — from npm
- `sections@0.1.0`, `i18n@0.2.0`, `client-kit@0.1.0` — from `.packages/*.tgz`

### Integrations Wired
- **HubSpot**: Contact form POSTs to `api.hsforms.com` with rate limiting + graceful fallback
- **Mailchimp**: `/api/subscribe` route with idempotent PUT + graceful degradation
- **GA4**: Measurement ID `G-XE49GLEP34` in env + DNS in CSP, gtag via Script in layout
- **Admin panel**: Dashboard at `/admin` with `AdminGuard` component (Supabase session check)
- **Content honesty**: Stats → `[Datos reales pendientes]`, testimonials → placeholders, TeamSection badges `⚠️ IA`

### Docker Build (current)
```bash
# From VPS
cd /root/nexa-paraguay
git pull origin main
docker build --no-cache -t nexa-paraguay:prod .
docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web
```

## App Router Architecture (current)

- Framework: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript 5, Tailwind v4
- Routes use `[locale]/[slug]` pattern — 4 locales (es/en/nl/de), 25+ slug pages
- Content JSON-driven: `content/{locale}.json` (4 locales, `es.json` is source of truth at ~208KB), `nexa-pages/*.json` (26+ page configs), `images.json` (image registry)
- SectionsRenderer: `'use client'` component with 30+ sections loaded via `dynamic(() => import(...), { ssr: true })`
- PPR disabled (`cacheComponents` removed) — all routes are `● SSG` static HTML
- Each section component receives `pageContent` (the resolved content sub-object) and `data` (same, as fallback)
- Section content keys in page configs reference dot-paths into the locale JSON (e.g. `"home.hero"`)
- Dynamic routes using `generateStaticParams()` — all pages pre-rendered at build time
- Docker Swarm deployment with standalone output

### SectionsRenderer Content Resolution

**Critical rule for content keys in nexa-pages/*.json:**

Components in `sections.tsx` access `pageContent.KEY` (e.g., `HeroSection` does `pageContent.hero`).
Components in `FeedbackSection` use `data` directly (e.g., `data.title`).

| Component | What it reads | Correct content key |
|-----------|--------------|-------------------|
| HeroSection | `pageContent.hero` | `"home"` |
| TrustSection | `pageContent.trust` | `"home"` |
| ServicesSection | `d.services?.groups \|\| d.groups` | `"home"` |
| WhyCountrySection | `pageContent.whyCountry` | `"home"` |
| ProcessSection | `pageContent.process` | `"home"` |
| FeedbackSection | `data.title`, `data.eyebrow` | `"home.feedback"` |
| CtaBanner | `pageContent.finalCta \|\| pageContent.cta` | `"home"` |

If a section returns null, check the content key depth against what the component reads.

## Build & Deploy (current)

```bash
# Full clean rebuild (REQUIRED for SSG changes)
cd /root/nexa-paraguay
git pull origin main
docker build --no-cache -t nexa-paraguay:prod .
docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web
sleep 5
curl -sL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://nexa.paragu-ai.com
curl -sL -o /dev/null -w " admin:%{http_code}" https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/admin
echo ""
```

**CRITICAL — Always `docker build --no-cache`.** Without `--no-cache`, Docker may reuse the builder stage layer which contains the old `.next/` output even if the source files have been updated.

## Integration: HubSpot Contact Form

The contact form at `/es/contacto` POSTs to `/api/contact`, which:
1. Rate-limits by IP (10 requests/hour)
2. Sends fields (name, email, phone, message, program) to HubSpot API
3. Falls back to console.log if HubSpot is unreachable
4. Returns `{ ok: true }` regardless (never exposes errors to user)

**To verify:**
```bash
curl -s -X POST https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/api/contact \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"Test","email":"[REDACTED_EMAIL]","message":"Test submission"}'
# Response: {"ok":true,"remaining":9,"hubspot":"submitted"} or "logged"
```

## Integration: Mailchimp Subscribe

The newsletter form POSTs to `/api/subscribe`, which:
1. Uses Mailchimp API v3 PUT (idempotent — safe to retry)
2. Creates subscriber with status `subscribed`
3. Gracefully handles "already subscribed" (returns ok)
4. Falls back to console.log on API failure

**Required env var:** `MAILCHIMP_AUDIENCE_ID` and `MAILCHIMP_API_KEY` in `.env.local`

## Admin Panel

Located at `/admin`. Self-contained `AdminGuard` component:
1. Creates Supabase client inline (no `@ai-whisperers/auth` dependency)
2. Checks session via `sb.auth.getSession()`
3. Redirects to `/` if no session
4. Shows stats cards for pages/content/blogs

**On first deploy**, you need a Supabase user with admin role to log in.

## SSG Cache Invalidation

Key insight from this session: **all pages produce `BAILOUT_TO_CLIENT_SIDE_RENDERING`** in their SSG HTML `<main>` tags. This is the normal behavior of a `'use client'` SectionsRenderer wrapping dynamic imports — content renders on the client via RSC hydration, not in static HTML. See the generic pitfall in `nextjs-docker-swarm` skill (pitfall #22) for the full explanation and the two fix paths (static imports vs accepting RSC serialization).

Next.js 16 SSG + Docker Swarm creates **four layers of caching** that must all be cleared:

### 1. Docker layer caching
The builder stage runs `npm run build` inside it. `--no-cache` forces Docker to re-run the builder.
```bash
docker build --no-cache -t nexa-paraguay:prod .
```

### 2. Cloudflare/edge CDN cache
```bash
curl -sL -H "Cache-Control: no-cache" https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/en/contacto
```

### 3. Browser cache
Use incognito or hard-refresh when testing.

### Verification inside container
```bash
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=nexa_web) cat /app/.next/server/app/en/contacto.html | grep "expected content"
```

## CRITICAL PITFALL: JSON Corruption

The `patch` tool and `sed` can corrupt large JSON files (content/es.json is 208KB, 900+ lines). A missing comma or broken key breaks the entire site (content: {} empty response — all pages render as header+footer only). **Always validate after every JSON edit:**

```bash
python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('content/es.json')); print('VALID')"
```

## .npmrc Token Management

The `.npmrc` for GitHub Packages must have a valid classic PAT (`ghp_*`). Fine-grained PATs (`github_pat_*`) do NOT work with `npm.pkg.github.com`.

**To update a stale token:**
```bash
gh auth token
# Copy the output
sed -i 's|ghp_.*|<paste token>|' .npmrc
```

**To check if the current token works:**
```bash
NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG=.npmrc npm view @ai-whisperers/auth version --registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
```

## Locale Configuration Pattern

**File:** `src/lib/locales.ts` — single source of truth for locale constants.

```typescript
export const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] as const
export const DEFAULT_LOCALE = 'es'
export const LOCALE_COOKIE = 'NEXT_LOCALE'
export type Locale = (typeof LOCALES)[number]
export const LOCALE_FLAGS: Record<string, string> = {
  es: '/images/flags/es.svg',
  en: '/images/flags/en.svg',
  nl: '/images/flags/nl.svg',
  de: '/images/flags/de.svg',
}
```

**tsconfig.json must use `./src/*` path:**
```json
"paths": { "@/*": ["./src/*"] }
```

## Content Architecture: Homepage vs Dedicated Pages

**Critical rule: NO duplicated content between homepage and dedicated pages.**

Each piece of content has ONE owner:

| Content | Owner Page | Homepage Shows |

| Programs tiers | /programas | Summary headline + CTA link |
| Services groups | /servicios | Summary headline + CTA link |

Covers page config → section component mapping, generic fallback renderers, data key standardization, theming, and architectural cleanup for content-heavy Next.js Pages Router sites.", "tags": ["nextjs", "content", "audit", "architecture", "section-components", "theming", "standardization"], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
